examples: All the people in the party were disgusted with his bawdy jokes.
They tell bawdy Chaucerian tales, and show their crooked, gappy teeth like horses when they laugh.
Bold lipstick and bawdy dancing are the trademarks of Nida Chaudhry, who performs to packed houses at Lahore's Al Falah theater.
Like other black churches, Trinity's services are full of raucous laughter and sometimes bawdy humor.
Soon it sounded like a bawdy shanty, accompanied by cheeky laughs from the men.
